agent_notes: Quality ceiling is below the 30-35 target: Hammersmith and Fulham has strong riverside pubs, Arts & Crafts museums and markets, but few true Books & Coffee venues and only Ravenscourt Park from the PAINTS-approved Creative Walk safety list.
Several operational details, especially group walk-ins for 8-12 and exact July 2026 private-hire closures, are not publicly explicit and are marked UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
Museum/gallery choices lean local and craft/heritage-led rather than blockbuster press-led; Emery Walker's House is capped at Tier B because tours are pre-booked and small.

venues.opening_hours: Official site says open today 11:00-23:00, food 12:00-22:00; CAMRA lists Mon-Tue 12:00-23:00, Wed-Sat 11:00-23:00, Sun 12:00-22:30. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm event-date private hire.
venues.walk_in_policy: Bookings are available; pub format suggests walk-ins for drinks, but group walk-in for 8-12 is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good if using early evening or upstairs River Room; 8-12 seated without booking is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.capacity_notes: Traditional downstairs pub plus refurbished River Room; riverside peak and match days can tighten capacity.
venues.best_time_slot: Thursday 17:00-19:00 or Saturday 15:00-17:00 before dinner peak.
venues.why_fits_paints: A 300-year riverside pub gives strangers an immediate object to talk around: river traffic, old wood, Holst lore and Hammersmith Bridge. It is historic without feeling like a museum stop. The view and interior texture make it one of the borough's strongest Show & Tell rooms.
venues.anecdote_story: The pub says it was first licensed in 1722; local histories connect the room with Gustav Holst composing parts of the Hammersmith Suite.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Ale, wine, pub food and riverside seating.
venues.safety_notes: Lower Mall is quiet but close to Hammersmith Bridge Road and Hammersmith station; keep arrivals/departures on the lit riverside-to-station route.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 6 min walk by Google Maps search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Riverside demand and match days; host should call before publishing.

venues.opening_hours: CAMRA lists daily noon-23:00; official Fuller's page supplies history but event-date opening and private hire are UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Pub accepts visitors; group walk-in for 8-12 is operationally fragile because the historic rooms are small. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Possible only off-peak or outside; likely to split at peak. Tier B for capacity risk.
venues.capacity_notes: Famously small bar and compact rooms; better as atmospheric backup than primary for a larger group.
venues.why_fits_paints: The Dove is almost too obvious, but the room has rare narrative density: writers, river, Morris, and the smallest-bar mythology. It fits a Show & Tell brief beautifully for a smaller table. For PAINTS, the constraint is capacity rather than character.
venues.anecdote_story: The pub's history page evokes Regency writers and pre-Raphaelites; public histories link it to James Thomson, William Morris next door, and Doves Press naming.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Fuller's pub drinks, food and riverside terrace.
venues.safety_notes: Quiet riverside stretch; use Hammersmith Bridge Road or King Street for late departures.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 6 min walk by Google Maps search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Capacity and tourist/history obviousness penalty.

venues.neighbourhood_station: Black Lion Lane / Stamford Brook or Hammersmith
venues.opening_hours: Official site lists bar Mon-Sat 12:00-23:00 and Sun 12:00-22:00; food has separate lunch/dinner hours. CAMRA last updated 2026-05-01. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm event-date private hire.
venues.walk_in_policy: Bookings available; neighbourhood pub/bar should accept walk-ins, but group of 8-12 is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for early bar seating; call ahead for a table cluster.
venues.capacity_notes: Bar and restaurant with weekend breakfast; likely more robust than the tiny riverside pubs.
venues.why_fits_paints: It has the village-pub feel Hammersmith does well: not flashy, but full of warm wood, seasonal food and local regularity. It is less story-heavy than The Dove, but far easier to operate. That makes it a strong practical A-tier drinks pick.
venues.anecdote_story: CAMRA notes the site has traded under earlier names including Lazy Vine and Le St Pierre and is listed as an Asset of Community Value.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Seasonal pub food, beer, wine and bar snacks.
venues.safety_notes: Black Lion Lane is residential; route via King Street or Stamford Brook after dark.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 4 min walk by Google Maps search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Group walk-in must be confirmed; otherwise hold as early-evening only.

venues.opening_hours: Official booking page lists Monday closed, Tuesday 17:00-23:00, Wednesday 17:00-23:00, Thursday-Saturday 12:00-23:00 and Sunday 12:00-22:00/20:00 kitchen; CAMRA aligns on current pub hours. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm event-date private hire.
venues.walk_in_policy: Bookings available; bar walk-ins likely, but group of 8-12 is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good if early and with heads-up call.
venues.capacity_notes: Backstreet neighbourhood pub; avoid peak Sunday lunch and Friday dinner.
venues.why_fits_paints: A properly local Brackenbury pub gives the event a softer, less obvious west-London tone. It has enough food-and-drink seriousness without becoming a formal restaurant. The wisteria/neighbourhood texture is very PAINTS-coded.
venues.anecdote_story: CAMRA describes it as an 1853 backstreet local with historic interior interest.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Pub drinks, wine and generous British cooking.
venues.safety_notes: Quiet residential streets; route attendees to Hammersmith via main roads after dark.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 9 min walk by Google Maps search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Closed Mondays; small-room group tolerance needs a call.

venues.neighbourhood_station: Fulham riverside / Barons Court or Hammersmith
venues.opening_hours: Official site lists Mon-Sat 12:00-23:00 and Sunday shown as 12:00-02:45 in indexed snippet, with food times listed separately; Visit London lists Tue-Sat 12:00-23:00 and Sun to 22:30. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm current Sunday anomaly and event-date hours.
venues.walk_in_policy: Bookings available; terrace/pub walk-ins likely, but group of 8-12 is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good if outside peak or using the terrace; match/riverside days can be busy.
venues.capacity_notes: Large riverside pub with terrace; more spacious than Upper Mall pubs.
venues.why_fits_paints: The Crabtree is not hidden, but the river terrace and relaxed scale make it a useful Show & Tell fallback. It has enough Fulham texture to avoid feeling generic. It is Tier B because the story is thinner and the public hours need host confirmation.
venues.anecdote_story: The venue presents itself as Fulham's iconic riverside pub between Hammersmith and Putney.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Pub food, wine, beer, terrace and river views.
venues.safety_notes: Riverside path is pleasant by day; after dark route via main roads toward Hammersmith/Barons Court.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 8 min walk by Google Maps search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Sunday-hours discrepancy and group walk-ins must be checked.

venues.opening_hours: Official site publishes menus, booking and venue information; exact opening-hours page was not reliably indexed in search result. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Official copy says pop in for a pint and booking is available; 8-12 group walk-in is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely suitable off-peak; sports screens can change the room dynamic.
venues.capacity_notes: Backstreet pub with garden, Thai kitchen, darts/board games and functions information.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is a warm backup rather than a pristine tastemaker pick: hidden backstreet, Thai kitchen, games and a real local feel. It gives the Carpenters Arms route a nearby operational alternative. The sports-screen emphasis keeps it Tier B.
venues.anecdote_story: The pub markets itself as Hammersmith's hidden gem with Thai food, darts, board games and a roaring fire.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Pub drinks, Thai kitchen, games and garden.
venues.safety_notes: Residential backstreet; use King Street or Stamford Brook for late onward travel.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 4 min walk by Google Maps search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Check opening hours and avoid major sports fixtures.

venues.opening_hours: Official site lists Mon-Thu 17:00-23:00 and Fri-Sun 12:00-23:00. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm event-date private hire.
venues.walk_in_policy: Reservations available; walk-in group of 8-12 is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely suitable early evening, but candlelit pub scale needs a call.
venues.capacity_notes: 200-year-old local pub with open fires and candles; not a high-capacity bar.
venues.why_fits_paints: A characterful Victorian-feeling local near Barons Court, useful as an intimate fallback for the riverside cluster. It is more pub-comfort than cultural discovery. Tier B reflects thinner press and group uncertainty.
venues.anecdote_story: The venue describes itself as a 200-year-old Fulham local institution.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Pub food, beers, ales and wines.
venues.safety_notes: Residential but close to Barons Court and main roads; avoid late canal/river detours.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 8 min walk by Google Maps search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Smaller pub; confirm seating before publishing.

venues.neighbourhood_station: Fulham / West Brompton or Fulham Broadway
venues.opening_hours: Council page lists Monday-Saturday 08:00-18:00; Visit London confirms the same. Open-access market; event-date trader mix UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access street market; meeting point walk-in at a nearby independent cafe is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good as a browsing route; not seated. Meet away from stall pinch points.
venues.capacity_notes: Busy working street market; choose a clear meeting point near West Brompton/Fulham Broadway end.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is the borough's everyday market spine rather than a curated lifestyle market, which makes it valuable. It has colour, food, people-watching and enough friction to spark conversation. It also balances the prettier riverside picks with a working Fulham texture.
venues.anecdote_story: H&F describes the six-day market as a busy road with a constant stream of shoppers looking for bargains and fresh food.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Fruit, veg, household goods, street food and rotating traders.
venues.safety_notes: High-footfall daytime route; keep group together at crossings and avoid late market close.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 15 min by bus/walk combination; within market backup rule, verify on event day.
venues.risks_watchouts: Atmosphere varies by trader day and weather; meeting cafe must be separately checked.

venues.opening_hours: Official site says every Sunday 10:00-15:00 at Thomas's Academy, New King's Road; Instagram snippet also confirms Sunday trading. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm school-site exceptions.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access market; nearby independent coffee meeting point within 5 min is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for browsing and grazing; no fixed seating assumed.
venues.capacity_notes: School-site weekly farmers market with produce and street food.
venues.why_fits_paints: Producer focus is clear: naturally grown fruit and veg, organic/free-range meat, fish, cheese, bread and street food. It gives Market Days a specific Sunday rhythm. The New King's Road location is softer and more conversational than North End Road.
venues.anecdote_story: The market says it has moved to a new New King's Road home and is bigger, with independent farmers, bakers and cheesemongers.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Farm produce, bread, cheese, fish, charcuterie, baked goods and street food.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime school/road setting; use New King's Road crossings carefully.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 15 min by transit/walk combination; verify on Google Maps for exact meeting point.
venues.risks_watchouts: Weather and school-site exceptions; market previously has naming overlap with closed LFM Parson's Green listing, so use the current official Fulham Farmers Market site.

venues.opening_hours: Instagram snippet says every Saturday 09:30-15:00; market PDF/history sources list Saturday 09:00/10:00-15:00 and Wednesday openings. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm current Wednesday status.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access market; independent meeting point within 5 min is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for an outdoor Saturday browse with food stops.
venues.capacity_notes: Primary-school market setting; choose a gate meeting point and avoid school bottlenecks.
venues.why_fits_paints: Brook Green is more curated and maker-producer coded than the council weekday markets. It gives the group grazing, small traders and a pleasant west-London residential context. Strong enough for Tier A, with live-hours caveat.
venues.anecdote_story: Market materials frame it as shopping directly from people who grow, rear, catch, bake and make food.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Farm produce, bakery, street food and rotating artisan traders.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime residential/school setting; good transport links via Hammersmith and Kensington Olympia.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 14-15 min walk; market backup rule allows 15 min.
venues.risks_watchouts: Current Wednesday operation is uncertain; use Saturday unless host confirms.

venues.opening_hours: H&F says Hammersmith Food Market runs every Thursday 08:00-17:00; trader pages often state 10:00-15:00 for individual stalls. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm trader mix.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access market; meeting point cafe walk-in for 8-12 is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for lunch browsing; little seated dwell time unless paired with Lyric/Riverside cafe.
venues.capacity_notes: Civic square beside Lyric Hammersmith, strong transport but office-lunch peak.
venues.why_fits_paints: Operationally easy and centrally placed, but less romantic than Brook Green or Fulham Farmers. It works when PAINTS needs a quick weekday lunch-market format. Tier B because it is more functional than distinctive.
venues.anecdote_story: Council copy lists handmade pasta, Caribbean salads, hog roast and Thai food as examples of the Thursday food-market mix.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Street food and lunch traders.
venues.safety_notes: High-footfall transport hub; good for daytime but manage crowding around the Broadway.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 15 min by Hammersmith & City line from Hammersmith to Wood Lane plus walk; verify live.
venues.risks_watchouts: Small market and trader variability; not enough craft/maker identity for Tier A.

venues.opening_hours: H&F says Monday-Friday 08:00-15:00. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm trader mix and weather plan.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access food market; independent coffee meeting point within 5 min is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for a short lunch market, weaker for a 2-3 hour PAINTS format.
venues.capacity_notes: Weekday food-market setup serving local workers.
venues.why_fits_paints: Useful geographic counterweight for White City, but it reads more as lunch infrastructure than cultural destination. It clears the floor through access, food and local utility. Keep it as a Tier B operational option.
venues.anecdote_story: Council copy says the market serves a growing number of customers looking for something different and tasty.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Street food/lunch traders.
venues.safety_notes: High-footfall White City corridor; avoid pairing with Westfield interior as PAINTS venue.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 15 min by transit; verify live.
venues.risks_watchouts: Too functional; do not use as a standalone flagship.

venues.opening_hours: Public park on PAINTS Safety-First Shortlist; H&F park page should be checked for current opening/works before event. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm any closures.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access park route; weather backup cafe/pub walk-in is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for a daytime 60-90 minute creative walk with three or four stops.
venues.capacity_notes: Use daytime only; keep stops near high-footfall paths and exits.
venues.why_fits_paints: It is the only PAINTS-approved park route inside the borough, so it carries the Creative Walk category locally. The route can use lake/garden views, the old house footprint, Paddenswick Road edges and a Ravenscourt-to-Brackenbury finish. It is safe-list compliant without pretending H&F has more approved green routes than it does.
venues.anecdote_story: Route prompts: the former Ravenscourt House estate, the lake/garden remnants, the park's role as local common ground, and the contrast between King Street traffic and quiet residential edges.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Park landscape, lake, mature trees and neighbourhood architecture.
venues.safety_notes: Confirmed on PAINTS Safety-First Shortlist. Daytime route only; do not extend to Wormwood Scrubs, which is explicitly removed from the PAINTS list.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 5 min from east/south park edge; verify exact start point.
venues.risks_watchouts: Only one approved park in-borough; weather backup is pub/cafe rather than another walk.

venues.opening_hours: Official site lists museum and historic rooms daily 10:30-17:00; garden hours vary, with botanic garden 07:00-18:00 and walled garden 10:15-16:15. Free timed museum slot required; private room closures possible.
venues.walk_in_policy: Free museum visit uses booking/timeslot; walk-in availability for 8-12 is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm. Tier A retained because slots are free and operationally robust if checked.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for museum plus garden; host should book/free-check timeslots together.
venues.capacity_notes: Historic rooms, museum, garden and cafe; some rooms can close for private events.
venues.why_fits_paints: Fulham Palace gives PAINTS a rare mix of horticulture, power, colonial history and domestic texture in one compact visit. It is quieter than central museums and feels like a discovery even for Londoners. The garden exhibition/current interpretation makes it strong enough for a full Museum & Gallery Day.
venues.anecdote_story: The Palace was the Bishops of London's historic residence; current interpretation includes colonialism/transatlantic slavery resistance and garden histories.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Historic rooms, museum displays, botanic and walled gardens, temporary exhibitions.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime recommended; Bishop's Park itself is not on PAINTS Creative Walk list, so use palace/garden as venue context, not a standalone Creative Walk.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 8 min walk; cultural/bookish backup rather than museum-grade backup due sparse local museum density.
venues.risks_watchouts: Room closures and timed-slot availability must be checked one week before.

venues.opening_hours: Official site says Thursday-Sunday 10:00-16:00, last entry 15:30, admission £5; cashless. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm July event date and group entry.
venues.walk_in_policy: Official social snippet says book online ahead or just come in; group of 8-12 is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good if staggered in small rooms; call ahead for comfort.
venues.capacity_notes: Museum occupies coach house/lower rooms of Kelmscott House; compact but purposeful.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is one of Hammersmith's purest PAINTS venues: textiles, printing, domestic history and a story people can carry into conversation. It is specific, small and beautiful. The only constraint is room scale.
venues.anecdote_story: Morris lived at Kelmscott House from 1878 until 1896; the museum presents his home, artistry, family life and creative circle.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Original designs, printing blocks, personal memorabilia, textiles and Morris connections.
venues.safety_notes: Upper Mall route as for The Dove; use main-road/station route after dark.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 8 min walk by Google Maps search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Small rooms; not open Monday-Wednesday.

venues.opening_hours: Official exhibition page lists 2026 exhibitions; Visit London says pre-booked tours on Thursdays and Saturdays from March to end November. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm live ticket inventory.
venues.walk_in_policy: Pre-booked guided tours only; capped at Tier B under PAINTS rules. Group of 8-12 may exceed normal tour comfort and must be confirmed.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Fragile: tours are small and house has narrow stairs; not suitable for casual walk-ins.
venues.capacity_notes: Grade II* listed house with narrow stairs and low lighting; no step-free access.
venues.best_time_slot: Thursday or Saturday 11:00 or 13:00 tour if booked by host.
venues.why_fits_paints: A preserved Arts & Crafts interior is exactly the kind of intimate cultural space PAINTS should know about. The 2026 Emery Walker anniversary exhibition gives it current relevance. Operationally it is fragile, so it stays Tier B.
venues.anecdote_story: The 2026 exhibition 'Both a Figure and a Person' marks Walker's 175th anniversary and foregrounds his craft and friendships; the later 2026 show covers Walker and the private press movement.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Arts & Crafts interiors, Morris & Co material, typography/printing history and 2026 exhibition displays.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime route only; Hammersmith Terrace is quiet and residential.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 8 min walk.
venues.risks_watchouts: Reservation-only, small capacity, no step-free access.

venues.opening_hours: Riverside site lists many current events; Bitesize Art Exhibition runs 6 July-31 August and the wider 2026 festival 27 July-30 August. General daily building hours are UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Exhibition appears open-access/free gallery context, but group walk-in and exact hours are UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good if the exhibition is in public gallery spaces; weaker if the building is theatre/cinema busy.
venues.capacity_notes: Multi-arts building with cinema/theatre/cafe; public spaces can absorb a small group.
venues.why_fits_paints: Riverside is a credible Hammersmith cultural anchor, especially when the galleries are active. The Bitesize Art Exhibition gives a current visual-art reason to gather. It is Tier B because exact public-gallery hours and walk-in group details need confirmation.
venues.anecdote_story: Riverside's history spans arts centre, television studio and recent redevelopment; the 2026 Bitesize programme brings theatre, film, music and visual art into the building.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Emerging/established artists in the Bitesize Art Exhibition; film/theatre context.
venues.safety_notes: Close to Hammersmith station and lit main roads; avoid isolated riverside departures late.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 10 min walk.
venues.risks_watchouts: Art exhibition starts 2026-07-06, so do not schedule on 2026-07-04 without host verification.

venues.neighbourhood_station: King Street / Ravenscourt Park or Hammersmith
venues.opening_hours: POSK Gallery site lists opening hours 09:00-21:00, seven days a week; current event pages show rotating exhibitions. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm exact exhibition dates and private-event closures.
venues.walk_in_policy: Gallery appears free/open-access; group walk-in for 8-12 is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely good for a short gallery visit; pair with cafe/bar in POSK or King Street.
venues.capacity_notes: Cultural-centre gallery, not a large museum; event overlap possible.
venues.why_fits_paints: POSK gives the borough a Polish cultural lens and a rotating contemporary-art programme. It is less polished than a commercial gallery, which can be a strength for PAINTS. Tier B because exhibition-specific press is thin.
venues.anecdote_story: The gallery is part of the Polish Social and Cultural Association and frequently shows Polish artists across painting, graphics, photography, sculpture and installations.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Rotating contemporary art exhibitions, often Polish and diaspora-linked.
venues.safety_notes: King Street is a busy main road with good transit; late departures should use Ravenscourt Park/Hammersmith stations.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 10 min walk.
venues.risks_watchouts: Exact exhibition on event date must be confirmed; not all listings are current-year pages.

venues.opening_hours: Official FAQ lists Mon-Sat 10:00-18:00 and Sun 11:00-17:00. Cafe hours are older-source dependent and UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Bookshop walk-ins accepted; group of 8-12 sitting for two hours is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for browsing; seated cafe/group discussion capacity must be confirmed.
venues.capacity_notes: Independent bookshop with cafe references and basement/event-room references in press/blog sources.
venues.why_fits_paints: Nomad is the cleanest H&F Books & Coffee fit: real independent bookshop, cafe history and a long browsing identity. It can support a gentle two-hour literary event if seating is confirmed. It is more charming than performative.
venues.anecdote_story: Local and bookshop blogs describe it as an independent Fulham Road bookshop with a cafe, expert recommendations and a stay-awhile feel.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Books, recommendations, hot drinks/cafe offer to be confirmed.
venues.safety_notes: Fulham Road is busy and well served; use Parsons Green/Fulham Broadway routes.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 10 min by bus/walk or 18-20 min walk; no qualifying same-category backup within strict 10 min walk found.
venues.risks_watchouts: Cafe seating and 8-12 tolerance are binding; host must call before publishing.

venues.opening_hours: Official site says Mon-Fri 9am-ish-6pm-ish, Sat-Sun 10am-ish-6pm-ish and notes irregular opening hours; social/search snippets vary. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Bookshop walk-ins likely; cafe/coffee and seating for 8-12 are UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for browsing only; weak for seated two-hour stay unless cafe/space is confirmed.
venues.capacity_notes: Dense second-hand bookshop; atmospheric but not operationally smooth.
venues.why_fits_paints: The atmosphere is exceptional: stacks, serendipity and one of London's old-school second-hand bookshop stories. It barely clears Books & Coffee because coffee evidence is inconsistent. Use as a bookish wander/backup, not a high-confidence cafe event.
venues.anecdote_story: Time Out describes it as a chaotic second-hand-literature destination; local writeups identify proprietor Ray Cole and a huge nearby warehouse of books.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Second-hand, vintage and rare books; coffee/cafe offer UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.safety_notes: Close to Putney Bridge and Fulham High Street; daytime best.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 18-20 min walk; no qualifying same-category backup within strict 10 min walk found, so keep as Tier B.
venues.risks_watchouts: Irregular opening and weak cafe confirmation.

rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: Closed after nearly ten years according to current Instagram/Facebook posts; older RAW WINE/OpenTable pages are stale.
Instagram result says the venue closed after two years; operational status is too uncertain for PAINTS.
Operationally strong wine venue, but it is a multi-site brand/self-pour chain format and fails the independent PAINTS filter for Tier A.
Excellent and culturally validated gastropub, but Michelin/reservation-led dining and price posture make it unsuitable for walk-in Show & Tell Drinks for 8-12.

rejected_candidates.venue_name: Parson's Green Farmers' Market (London Farmers Markets listing)
rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: LFM page explicitly says the market is no longer open; replaced in this file by the current Fulham Farmers Market official site at Thomas's Academy.
Explicitly removed in PAINTS v6 safety list; cannot be sourced or used as backup no matter how locally relevant.
Adjacent to Fulham Palace and attractive, but not on the PAINTS-approved Creative Walk safety list; can support Fulham Palace context only, not a sourced Creative Walk.
Strong Books & Coffee format, but the Chelsea branch is outside Hammersmith and Fulham; not used to pad the borough.
